items.final_wording: Supports cognition, vision, hearing, swallowing, movement, continence, communication, nutrition, strength, sensation, balance, mood and mental health, pain, activity, participation, environment, medicines, allergies, goals, discharge information, and documented follow-up planning.

source.title: Stroke rehabilitation in adults
source.setting: Hospital, community, and long-term stroke rehabilitation and review.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult stroke rehabilitation assessment and longer-term review; not an exact source for isolated TIA follow-up and not directly validated for UAE services.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.14 — stroke assessment and rehabilitation plan

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports cognition, vision, hearing, swallowing, movement, continence, communication, nutrition, strength, sensation, balance, mood and mental health, pain, activity, participation, environment, medicines, allergies, goals, discharge information, and documented follow-up planning.

items.rationale: Supports multidisciplinary assessment and an entered rehabilitation plan for ICU mobility review; it does not generate a clinical decision.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting long-term-condition burden, day-to-day activity difficulty, support from multiple services, frailty or falls context, number of regular medicines, gait and self-reported health assessment when actually completed.

source.title: Multimorbidity: clinical assessment and management — Recommendations
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE geriatric and primary-care adaptation
source.population: Adults with two or more long-term conditions, including frailty, falls, sensory impairment, chronic pain, mental health conditions, and treatment burden.
source.setting: Primary and community care with person-centred multimorbidity review.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting day-to-day function, frailty and falls context, treatment burden, multiple medicines, personal goals, values, carer involvement, and clinician-led treatment review. It does not auto-score frailty or recommend starting, stopping, or changing treatment.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.4.4 — multimorbidity, daily activities, falls, medicines, and frailty context

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting long-term-condition burden, day-to-day activity difficulty, support from multiple services, frailty or falls context, number of regular medicines, gait and self-reported health assessment when actually completed.

items.rationale: Supports documented function, daily activities, falls and frailty context for ICU mobility review; it does not generate a clinical decision.
